name: Palco FCU
description_short: PALCO Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving eight Pennsylvania counties since 1938, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
description_long:
PALCO Federal Credit Union was established in 1938 and has operated as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution for over 85 years. The organization is dedicated to serving the financial needs of communities in Bradford, Columbia, Lycoming, Montour, Northumberland, Sullivan, Tioga, and Union counties in Pennsylvania. As a federally chartered credit union, PALCO is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and offers deposit insurance protection for member accounts.

PALCO FCU provides a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (checking, savings, money market, club accounts, and certificate accounts), consumer loans (auto, personal, student, and home equity), mortgages, VISA credit cards, and specialized products like Helper Loans and Loan Insurance. Members can access their accounts through multiple channels: online banking, a mobile app, shared branching networks, and ATM services via the Allpoint ATM locator. The credit union also offers financial tools such as bill pay, e-statements, a loan calculator, and financial advisor services.

PALCO distinguishes itself through its long community history, local focus on eight specific Pennsylvania counties, and commitment to member service at affordable rates. The organization emphasizes stability and growth while maintaining its mission to meet members' total financial needs. They offer unique member benefits including no-cost AD&D coverage, a Members Helping Members Scholarship program, and access to TruStage Insurance products. The credit union actively recruits talent and maintains an engaged community presence through social media and newsletters.

As a credit union, PALCO operates on a not-for-profit model, meaning earnings are returned to members through better rates and lower fees compared to many for-profit banks. However, membership eligibility is limited to residents of their eight-county service area, which may exclude potential members outside Pennsylvania. The organization's digital capabilities appear modern with mobile banking and online lending options, though specific product rates and fee schedules are not detailed on the public website.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Mobile banking app and online banking
Shared branching and ATM network access via Allpoint
Loan calculator and financial advisor services
pros: Not-for-profit credit union structure returns earnings to members through competitive rates
Long operational history since 1938 demonstrates stability and community commitment
NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 per account category
Multiple account access methods: online banking, mobile app, shared branching, and ATM network
Comprehensive product offering including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and credit cards
No-cost AD&D (accidental death and dismemberment) coverage for members
Members Helping Members Scholarship program for community support
Online lending and bill pay capabilities for modern banking convenience
cons: Service area limited to eight Pennsylvania counties, restricting membership eligibility
Website does not publish specific loan rates, APRs, or fee schedules for comparison
Limited information about credit card rewards, benefits, or terms on the public site
No details provided about minimum balance requirements or account maintenance fees
Unclear whether membership eligibility extends beyond county residents to employees or association members
best_for: Residents of Bradford, Columbia, Lycoming, Montour, Northumberland, Sullivan, Tioga, and Union counties in Pennsylvania
Members seeking a community-focused, not-for-profit financial institution with comprehensive lending and deposit products
Individuals preferring multi-channel banking access (online, mobile, branching, ATM networks)
Borrowers interested in auto loans, mortgages, personal loans, and student loans with competitive member rates
diagnosis: PALCO FCU is best suited for residents within its eight-county Pennsylvania service area who value a member-owned, not-for-profit institution with comprehensive banking and lending services. The main caveat is that membership is geographically restricted and specific product terms, rates, and fees are not disclosed on the public website, requiring direct contact with the credit union for rate and fee comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
